content.problemStatement:
**This is the hard version of the problem. Here, the values of XXX and KKK can go upto 10910^9109, and HiH_iHi​ can be as large as 101810^{18}1018.**

In a video game, you are tasked with defeating monsters. The iii-th monster has a health value of HiH_iHi​.

You start with an initial health value of XXX.

You can defeat the iii-th monster if your current health XXX is **strictly greater than** the monster's health HiH_iHi​, i.e, if X>HiX\gt H_iX>Hi​.

After defeating the iii-th monster, your health value changes to HiH_iHi​ - that is, set X=HiX = H_iX=Hi​.

Additionally, you have access to a spell that can multiply your current health by KKK, but you can use this spell **at most once** at any point during the game.

Find the maximum number of monsters you can defeat if you use the spell optimally.

Note:
You can defeat any monster only once.


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of two lines of input.

The first line of each test case contains three space-separated integers NNN, XXX, and KKK — the number of monsters, your initial health, and the spell's multiplier, respectively.
The second line contains NNN space-separated integers H1,H2,…,HNH_1, H_2, \ldots, H_NH1​,H2​,…,HN​, denoting the health of monsters.




Output Format
For each test case, output on a new line the maximum number of monsters you can defeat using the spell optimally.
constraints: The sum of NNN over all test cases won't exceed 5⋅1055 \cdot 10^55⋅105.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test case 111: Initially, X=3X = 3X=3. If we use the spell immediately, it changes to X⋅K=3⋅2=6X\cdot K = 3\cdot 2 = 6X⋅K=3⋅2=6.
Then, all 555 monsters can be defeated if we do it in the order [5,4,3,2,1][5, 4,3 , 2, 1][5,4,3,2,1].
Test case 222: Even if we multiply our health by KKK, it's impossible to defeat the monster since XXX must be strictly larger than HiH_iHi​.
Test case 333: Consider the following sequence of operations:

Since 3=X>A2=23 = X \gt A_2 = 23=X>A2​=2, defeat monster i=2i=2i=2. This will change XXX to 222.
Since 2=X>A1=12 = X \gt A_1 = 12=X>A1​=1, defeat monster i=1i=1i=1. This will change XXX to 111.
Now use the spell, multiplying XXX by K=2K = 2K=2 and making it 222.
Since 2=X>A3=12 = X \gt A_3 = 12=X>A3​=1, defeat monster i=3i=3i=3. This will change XXX to 111.

All three monsters have been defeated.
